stem: A caterer charges $18 per adult meal and $10 per child meal. A family is planning a party with a budget of no more than $500 for food. If there will be 15 adults at the party, what is the greatest number of children that can be invited?
explanation: Adult cost: $18 \times 15 = $270. Remaining budget: $500 - $270 = $230. Set up $10k \leq 230$, so $k \leq 23$. The greatest number of children is 23.

distractorRationale.A: Cluster B error: Calculated adult cost as $18 \times 15 = $260$ (arithmetic slip: $18 \times 15 = 260$ instead of 270), then $500 - 260 = 240$, and $240 \div 10 = 24$.
distractorRationale.C: Cluster D error: Ignored adult meal costs entirely and solved $10k \leq 500$, getting $k \leq 50$.
distractorRationale.D: Cluster C error: Correctly found $k \leq 23$ but then subtracted 3, reasoning that 'no more than $500' means they should stay well under budget, choosing 20.
